description_html:
RoboWars is a high-intensity combat robotics competition organized by the Robotics Club, IIITDM Kancheepuram. The event challenges participants to design, build, and operate a remote-controlled combat robot capable of competing in a tournament-style arena battle. Teams must combine mechanical design, electronics, control systems, and strategic thinking to dominate opponents and emerge victorious.

Event Objective:

  • The primary objective of RoboWars is to encourage hands-on learning and innovation in robotics by providing a real-world platform where teams test their engineering skills under competitive conditions. Robots battle head-to-head, with victory achieved through immobilization, superior control, aggression, and damage.

Competition Format:

  • Tournament-style knockout rounds
  • Each match lasts 3 minutes of active fight time
  • Teams are given 20 minutes of preparation time between matches
  • Maximum team size: 8 members

Robot Specifications (Summary):

  • Maximum weight: 8 kg
  • Maximum dimensions: 75 cm × 75 cm × 50 cm
  • Only wedge or fork-wedge type passive weapons allowed
  • Electric power only (no IC engines)
  • Maximum operating voltage: 24V DC
  • Pneumatic and hydraulic systems allowed under strict safety rules

Judging:

  • A match can be won by immobilizing the opponent for 10 seconds. If no immobilization occurs, judges decide the winner based on:
    • Damage (5 points)
    • Control (3 points)
    • Aggression (3 points)

Safety & Conduct:

  • Strict safety standards are enforced throughout the event. Unsafe robots, prohibited weapons, or unsportsmanlike behavior may lead to immediate disqualification. Teams must comply with all instructions from referees and officials at all times.

Who Should Participate?

  • RoboWars is ideal for students and robotics enthusiasts interested in mechanical design, electronics, embedded systems, and competitive engineering challenges.
